name: SCM Database Privileged Operation Detected
description: Detection of privileged operations on the SCM (Service Control Manager) database. This can indicate an attempt by an attacker to manipulate services or achieve persistence/defense evasion by interacting with service configurations. The detection specifically looks for read or write attempts on the SCM database, which are indicative of a process trying to enumerate, create, modify, or delete services.

prompts.prompt: Investigate the security event at `{timestamp}` on host `{hostname}` where a privileged operation on the SCM database was detected. The process `{process_name}` with Process ID `{process_id}` performed a `{operation_type}` operation on the SCM database. Analyze the command line (`{command_line}`) for any suspicious arguments and review the user (`{user}`) associated with the activity. Determine if this is a legitimate administrative action or a potential malicious attempt to manipulate services. Provide a summary of your findings and suggest immediate mitigation steps.

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: The hostname where the event was detected.

parameters.name: process_name
parameters.description: The name of the process that performed the privileged operation on the SCM database.

parameters.name: process_id
parameters.description: The Process ID of the process that performed the privileged operation on the SCM database.

parameters.name: operation_type
parameters.description: The type of operation performed on the SCM database (e.g., read, write, create, delete).

parameters.name: command_line
parameters.description: The full command line of the process that performed the privileged operation.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which the process was executed.
